briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was discovered to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of banking middleware firm Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit cards into new territory with novel rewards structures, credit models, and embedded features that challenge traditional banking norms.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and alternative credit scoring.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what it means for incumbents, fintechs, and consumers alike.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a foundational step still shaping the country's consumer-driven banking roadmap today.

- **[How new SR&ED changes could supercharge Canadian hard tech and manufacturing startups](https://betakit.com/how-new-sred-changes-could-supercharge-canadian-hard-tech-and-manufacturing-startups/)** — *BetaKit* — Recent reforms to Canada's Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax credit program could significantly boost funding available to Canadian tech and deep-tech startups.

- **[Canada's economy adds 14,000 jobs in March after February's whopping losses](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canadian-economy-march-2026-9.7158761?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's labour market showed modest recovery in March 2026, adding 14,000 jobs following significant losses the previous month.

- **[Hypertec becomes key partner for Nvidia in Canada](https://betakit.com/hypertec-becomes-key-partner-for-nvidia-in-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — Montreal-based Hypertec has been named a key Nvidia partner in Canada, positioning the company as a critical node in the country's growing AI infrastructure ecosystem.

- **[Former Alberta Innovates exec joins Toast as COO](https://betakit.com/former-alberta-innovates-exec-joins-toast-as-coo/)** — *BetaKit* — A former Alberta Innovates executive has been appointed COO of Toast, reflecting continued talent movement between Canada's public innovation sector and private tech industry.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative look at the darker consequences of fintech's rapid expansion, including financial exclusion, surveillance, and the risks of unchecked innovation — themes directly relevant to building an inclusive and responsible fintech ecosystem in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Apple unveils contactless payments via Tap to Pay on iPhone](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2022/02/apple-unveils-contactless-payments-via-tap-to-pay-on-iphone/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's Tap to Pay capability turns iPhones into payment terminals, a payments innovation with strong implications for small businesses and merchants across Atlantic Canada's SME-heavy economy.

- **[In a new CUSMA, should Canada offer the U.S. stronger energy rights?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/cusma-energy-proportionality-9.7159330?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — As Canada-U.S. trade negotiations intensify, the economic uncertainty created by CUSMA renegotiations has downstream implications for investment capital flows and fintech funding across all Canadian regions, including Atlantic Canada.
